events.title: Mississippi Law: Policing and Reform in America's Jim Crow Countryside
events.description: Justin Randolph explores rural law enforcement to explain US racial authoritarianism between the Civil War and the civil rights movement in Jim Crow Mississippi — from slave patrols to state highway patrols. Black Mississippians fought to defend their loved ones against paramilitary police reform. A genealogy of Jim Crow rule and the infrastructure of mass incarceration.

events.title: 'Films That Explode Like Grenades: Robert Kramer and the Search for a Radical Cinema'
events.description: A discussion with Whitney Strub celebrating his new book on Robert Kramer (1939–99), emblematic filmmaker of the late-1960s New Left, cofounder of leftist documentary collective Newsreel, and director of Ice, Milestones, and Route One/USA. An intimate history of 1960s radicalism and its generational legacies.

events.title: Creating Your Personal Narrative
events.description: The BGWP leads a workshop on crafting your personal narrative — brainstorming, workshop time, and a history of why individual stories matter.
